note: maker != checker: findings raised by independent reviewers that did not write the code. clean=true means no unresolved critical/high CODE defect; S1 is an operational secret-hygiene action for the owner (not a repo defect).

findings.issue: validateRecap team check was a no-op on the live path (MatchRecap passed no knownTeams), so a hallucinated team could pass if its digits were grounded.
findings.fix: recap() now fail-safes: a non-grounded (custom/LLM) provider without a team registry never displays — it falls back to the grounded template. Live path uses the grounded generator (cannot hallucinate). Registry-based team grounding is required and tested for the future LLM provider (FR-082).

findings.issue: factNumbers() mixed stage-label digits into the allowed set (R16→16, R32→32), letting a fabricated number equal to the stage slip past.
findings.fix: Number grounding is scoreline-only; stage labels are now digit-free (e.g. 'the last sixteen').

findings.issue: Injection detection is a phrase blocklist that a real LLM could evade.
findings.fix: Low blast radius today (grounded-only provider; raw output never shown; React escapes the text node). When FR-082 adds an LLM provider, replace the blocklist with strict allow-model grounding. Documented in ADR-0016.

findings.issue: gen-traceability counted @trace in ANY source file (comments/strings), so coverage could be faked and the ratchet would lock in fiction.
findings.fix: Scanner now only reads test/eval files (*_test.go, *.test.*, *.eval.*); a @trace in ordinary source no longer counts. Coverage held at 17/19 after the change.

findings.issue: Cycle guard (depth>8) could silently, order-dependently mis-order a malformed feeder graph.
findings.fix: Added a per-path `seen` set (deterministic: a repeat is treated as a root) and tightened MAX_DEPTH to the real tree height (5).

findings.issue: scoring/spec.md attributes FR-014 (bonus) to CAP-02 while it is implemented/traced in internal/resolve.
findings.fix: Added a locality note to scoring/spec.md; FR-014 is verified by resolve_test.go where it lives.

findings.issue: Score() probed against all spec scenarios — no off-by-one/sign/nil/purity break found.
The on-disk .env holds real production credentials. It is gitignored and absent from git history (verified) — not a repo leak — but the values were exposed to review tooling.
findings.fix: OWNER DECISION 2026-07-01: rotation deferred for now; secrets are NOT in git (gitignored + not in history). Not a deploy blocker; revisit later.

findings.issue: .env gitignored, never tracked, absent from history; no real secret value in any tracked file/test/fixture/CI.
All 5 tools read-only; strict schemas reject unknown params + bounds before fetch; no credential held/forwarded; reveal-lock enforced.
Fail-closed JWT_SECRET; DemoGate ordering gives anon 401 not 403; ro/rw gating correct; new Google users default 'none' under demo mode.
Recap rendered as an escaped React text child; no dangerouslySetInnerHTML; guardrail + fallback are defense-in-depth.
No untrusted github.event.* in run: blocks; least-privilege permissions; gitleaks full-history; Trivy fails HIGH/CRITICAL.
Shared http.Server WriteTimeout=10s truncates any SSE chat stream >10s (routine for flash + the classifier round-trip) — breaks the normal case in prod.
findings.fix: Chat handler extends the write deadline for THIS response only via http.NewResponseController(w).SetWriteDeadline(aiTimeout+5s); other endpoints keep the 10s cap. (ai.go)

findings.issue: A control-char-only message passed the handler length pre-check, then failed sanitize AFTER SSE headers → opaque 'ai_error' frame instead of a 400.
findings.fix: Exported gemini.Sanitize (SSOT) and validate BEFORE opening the stream → clean 400. Handler and StreamChat now agree on validity.

findings.issue: Client-supplied conversation history reached the model unsanitized + unclassified — an injection channel bypassing L0/L1 (control chars, oversized turns, forged role:model).
findings.fix: sanitizeHistory() applies L0 to every prior turn (strip control chars, rune-cap, drop empty, normalize role); evals TestSanitizeHistory + TestStreamChatSanitizesHistory. Forged role:model remains possible until server-side sessions — accepted limitation, documented in ADR-0017.

findings.issue: ADR-0017 claimed a chat 'no-prompt-leak' L3 that streaming makes impossible (tokens already on the wire).
findings.fix: ADR corrected to as-built: chat L3 is an output byte-cap only; full L3 validation applies to buffered cards; chat disclosure resistance rests on L1 + L2 anti-injection.

findings.issue: perUserLimiter map never evicts (unbounded-growth pattern).
findings.fix: Bounded in practice by the friends-pool user count (dozens); no TTL added to stay right-sized. Documented; add lazy eviction before any public deployment.

findings.issue: Evals didn't cover history hygiene, and the 'user text stays a separate turn, never in the system prompt' invariant lives only in vertex.go.
findings.fix: Added history-sanitization evals. The turn-vs-system-instruction wiring is in vertex.go (toContents/genConfig), integration-only by design (ADR-0017 concedes live-LLM paths aren't unit-tested).

findings.issue: System instruction was built with RoleUser, which can weaken its priority vs real user turns.
findings.fix: Now a role-less genai.Content (NewPartFromText), so the guardrail persona isn't labeled a user turn. (vertex.go)

findings.issue: nginx /api/ streaming relied solely on the backend's X-Accel-Buffering header; default 60s proxy_read_timeout only coincidentally exceeded the 30s budget.
findings.fix: Added a dedicated location /api/ai/ with proxy_buffering off + proxy_read_timeout 120s. (nginx.conf)

findings.issue: Backend runs a read-only rootfs; the WIF/ADC token exchange could fail on an unwritable /tmp at first call.
findings.fix: Overlay adds tmpfs /tmp for the backend. (docker-compose.gemini.yml)
